mentions.note: Reports Thursday after 8-week losing streak. Memory chip cost pressure on margins key watch. iPhone 17 strong launch should show in quarter. Gemini AI partnership may net $20B+ annually. Only Mag 7 company avoiding massive CapEx — own it don't trade it.
Great momentum but trades at one of highest P/E multiples in entire market. Too much danger from valuation; Cramer avoiding despite company quality.

Own it, don't trade it. 8-week losing streak but up $7.37 today on memory margin commentary. Watch gross margins, iPhone 17 durability, Gemini partnership ROI, and Siri reboot progress Thursday. Dodges massive CapEx rivals are burning.

#2 largest gold miner by market cap/production. Gold $5,000+ not bubble; geopolitical chaos and government spending sustaining. Tier-1 geology + tier-1 political stability crucial at these prices. 1.5M oz annual production growth over 5 years; 42-yr dividend increase streak; share buybacks ongoing.
Tower demand slowing; stock no longer as investable as when James Taiclet ran it. Sector headwinds outweigh dividend.
Developing own chips but massive Nvidia customer. Hyperscaler demand from Amazon enormous and growing — validates Nvidia ecosystem strength.
Down ~$100 from highs; Hock Tan delivers quarter after quarter on software, hardware, Google work expansion. Represents one of the cheaper AI plays with expanding margins.
Caller held outsized position; Cramer prefers Nucor as superior operator. No specific sell but Nucor better-positioned in steel.

Trading at 50x earnings (vs historical high of 47x); re-up rate weak but numbers coming through. Stock bounced on technicals after pantry-stocking discount already priced in. Stay long despite valuation concerns.
Preferred midstream energy / LNG transport play over MVGS. Stronger structural position in energy infrastructure.

First major 2026 IPO; priced $24.50, opened $28.50, closed Friday $32.56 (+33%). Disruptive asset-light equipment rental with proprietary T3 software platform and OWN sale-leaseback model. 140% revenue CAGR 2015–2024; 36% 3-year CAGR justifies 14.5x EV/EBITDA vs. peers' 8–10x. Similar playbook to early Carvana — buy on weakness.

IPO up 33% on debut (priced $24.50, closed $32.56). Asset-light T3 software + OWN sale-leaseback model enables rapid organic expansion. 140% revenue CAGR 2015–24; 36% 3-yr CAGR vs 12% (United Rentals), 17% (Herc). EV/EBITDA 14.5x justified by growth and disruption. Buy small, add on weakness.
Advertising heavily in sports (football); selling yield story. Midstream energy infrastructure play.
Emergency generator maker fell after rallying pre-storm on supply constraints; plummeted 11 points when power outages were lighter than expected. Event-driven, not fundamental.
Announced own chips but remains gigantic Nvidia customer. Demand from hyperscalers (Google, Amazon, Microsoft) enormous and growing — validates Nvidia's secular position.
Benefited from storm emergency buying; stock rose slightly despite blizzard (event already discounted). Solid counter-cyclical play but no new catalyst.

Reports Wednesday. Core ad business strong but CapEx guidance will dominate reaction — 2026 spend forecast even higher than 2025. Need clarity on ROI from multi-billion-dollar AI investments. Stock down 16% from August highs; catalyst required for rally.

Trading <23x earnings after 16% decline from August highs. Strong ad numbers won't matter if 2026 CapEx guidance disappoints again. Catalyst needed on CapEx or AI ROI clarity. Watch Wednesday earnings.
Terrific inexpensive stock for income generation. Positioned for older investors seeking yield, not growth.
Terrific inexpensive stock from Cramer's book. Ideal for income-focused (older) investors seeking steady dividend yield.

Reports Wednesday. Watch CapEx guidance (no incremental increases desired) and Azure growth upside (39% vs. expected 36%). Copilot and Windows moat questions linger. Down 13% since last report; need reassurance on spending trajectory.

Down 13% since October report after CapEx surprise reversal (now expects growth higher FY2026 vs FY2025). Need no new CapEx increases and Azure upside (39% vs 36% expected) plus Copilot/Windows enterprise clarity Wednesday.
Doing well, not expensive. Cramer prefers Enterprise Product Partners, Oneok, Energy Transfer as alternative yield plays in LNG/energy transport space.
Cramer's favorite steel company vs. Cleveland Cliffs; superior operator under CEO Leon Topalian. Stock hit 52-week high heading into session; buyer on any weakness.

$2B CoreWeave investment validates demand ('incredible' per Jensen). Bears unconvinced despite clear fundamentals. Own it, don't trade it; sellers will be wrong long-term. Allocation scarcity, not demand, is the issue CoreWeave helps solve.

Invested $2B in CoreWeave at $87.20/share, validating demand and preferred-partner status. Jensen Huang: demand incredible, applications through roof. CoreWeave deal proves ecosystem health, not vendor financing. Despite daily battleground noise, sellers repeatedly proven wrong. Own don't trade — patience pays.
Preferred midstream/LNG transport play. Strong structural exposure to energy infrastructure.

Insurer held since 2021 at $18; up to $46 then hammered to $38 post-earnings. Cramer bullish long-term despite poor quarter — insurers broadly missing this cycle. Keep buying, reinvesting dividends; many good quarters ahead.
Nearly half of sales overseas; weak dollar will boost competitiveness and earnings. Went up gigantically after soft quarter, positioning for margin recovery.

Flash storage arrays for hyperscaler data centers; $400M buyback and potential big customer deal. Sells at extremely high P/E; Cramer concerned interest rate spike will crush growth stocks regardless of earnings.

Owner of Kay, Zales, Jared. Elevated gold and silver prices may pressure margins; stock at 9x earnings may price in commodity upside already. High precious metals prices hurt jewelry retailer profitability.
Owner of Kay, Zales, Jared. Trading at ~9x earnings but high gold/silver prices may hurt jewelry demand. Hard to relate pricing to broader market dynamics.
Reports Wednesday but trades on narrative not numbers. EV business weak. Musk credibility on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (2027 launch) paramount — needs concrete progress updates.

Doesn't trade on EV fundamentals (business in bad shape); trades on Musk storytelling. Need clarity on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (promised end-2027 but history of timeline misses). Report Wednesday.
